description_long: This object is a preserved crab specimen mounted on a flat, rectangular backing. The crab displays a robust, rounded carapace with two prominent, upward-curving claws (chelae) and eight segmented walking legs radiating outward. The coloration is a mix of orange, tan, and brown, with the claws and legs showing a gradient from darker tips to lighter bases. The ventral side reveals the crab's segmented abdomen tucked beneath the carapace, and the texture of the exoskeleton appears hard and slightly rough. The eyes are positioned on short stalks at the front of the carapace, and the antennae are not clearly visible. The mounting board is a neutral, textured surface, and a color calibration cube is present for scale.
description: A mounted crab specimen with a hard, orange-brown exoskeleton, prominent claws, and eight legs displayed on a rectangular backing.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing the crab's carapace, both large claws raised, and all legs splayed outward.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the crab and the upward curve of the claws, with legs visible in profile.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the full width of the crab and the segmentation of the legs and abdomen beneath the carapace.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the claws and legs visible in profile, and the color calibration cube more prominent.
